articles.title: YOU DON'T ACTUALLY NEED 8 DIFFERENT AI TOOLS
articles.summary: Product teams are drowning in AI subscriptions: one tool for search, another for meeting notes, a third for research. None of them understand your company's context, and switching between them kills productivity.

articles.title: behind-the-scenes info): watch the recorded webinar
IS THIS AI'S HARDWARE MOMENT? A RELUCTANT DEBATE
articles.summary: AI's full potential won't be unlocked in a text box - it needs context, mobility, and seamless integration into daily life. Hardware, if purpose-driven and socially acceptable, may be the key to making that leap.

articles.title: AI PROTOTYPING: HOW 11 REAL-WORLD TEAMS ARE TRANSFORMING THEIR WORK WITH LOVABLE
articles.summary: AI prototyping tools like Lovable are transforming product discovery by enabling teams to quickly test ideas, visualize solutions, and align stakeholders - without sacrificing speed, clarity, or collaboration.

articles.title: BAN CHATGPT
articles.summary: Chatbots risk creating dependence and distorting reality. We must set limits before the harm outweighs the benefit.

articles.title: WHY STAKEHOLDER MANAGEMENT FAILS (AND WHAT WORKS INSTEAD)
articles.summary: Build alignment through clear communication, defined roles, and collaborative prioritization. Focus on relationships, not requests, and shift from pleasing to impactful decision-making.

articles.title: A COMPLETE GUIDE TO REVERSE-ENGINEERING STRATEGY
articles.summary: Most teams misunderstand product strategy - it's not your mission, goals, or roadmap, but the real choices driving behavior. By reverse-engineering what your team actually does, you uncover your true strategy and gain the clarity needed to align decisions, spot opportunities, and win.

articles.title: THE OKR PARALLEL UNIVERSE SYNDROME
articles.summary: OKRs fail when companies add side priorities. Treat OKRs as the single source of focus and update them instead of working around them.

articles.title: NO PAIN, NO GAIN
articles.summary: Growth happens through discomfort - physically, mentally, and creatively - by pushing beyond comfort zones with consistency. Intentional challenge builds strength, cognitive resilience, and unlocks change we can't foresee at the start.

articles.title: ON DEMAND LEARNING
articles.summary: AI lets anyone build fast, but real professionalism means learning deeply and building responsibly.

articles.title: TWO POM TRANSFORMATION FLAVORS
articles.summary: Scale-ups need to evolve beyond chaotic growth by linking product strategy to business goals, while enterprises must dismantle legacy systems and funding models to enable real product agility.

articles.title: GPT WRAPPERS
articles.summary: “Wrappers” aren't inherently bad - it's only a problem if they're easily replaceable.
